ID:       fizeau-458f10b0
Title:    openrouter health gate: add filter reasons and provider-eligibility plumbing in routing engine
Type:     task
Status:   closed
Priority: 1
Labels:   kind:bug,  area:routing,  area:provider-health,  impact:reliability,  parent:fizeau-0bc93861, ac-quality:needs-refinement
Desc:     Foundation for parent fizeau-0bc93861. Adds the four new FilterReason constants the openrouter health gate needs (credential_missing, credential_invalid, credit_exhausted, provider_unreachable) and plumbs a routing.Inputs map keyed by provider name into the engine candidate loop, so any provider can be marked eligible:false with a typed FilterReason and freshness timestamp without baking openrouter specifics into the engine. No openrouter wiring yet — that lands in the credential-check and credit-probe children.
Accept:   1. internal/routing/engine.go declares FilterReasonCredentialMissing, FilterReasonCredentialInvalid, FilterReasonCreditExhausted, FilterReasonProviderUnreachable as FilterReason values with the documented snake_case string forms ('credential_missing','credential_invalid','credit_exhausted','provider_unreachable').
2. routing.Inputs has a new map field (e.g. ProviderEligibilityOverrides map[string]ProviderEligibilityOverride{FilterReason, ProbeAt}) that the engine consults inside buildHarnessCandidates after the existing ProbeUnreachable gate. When a provider key matches, the candidate is set eligible=false with the typed FilterReason from the map and Reason populated with a stable human-readable string.
3. An explicit provider/harness/model pin bypasses the override exactly like the existing endpoint_unreachable gate.
4. Engine-level unit test in internal/routing/ (e.g. credential_health_gate_test.go) asserts:
   a. Each of the four new FilterReasons gates a matching provider when no pin is present.
   b. Explicit provider pin bypasses each gate.
   c. Map is nil-safe and absent providers are unaffected.
5. cd /Users/erik/Projects/fizeau && go test ./internal/routing/... green and full go test ./... green.
6. No regression in service_adr005_test.go or service_routing_test.go.

ID:       fizeau-73a00a6b
Title:    openrouter health gate: credential_missing/credential_invalid when api_key is unset or a literal env placeholder
Type:     task
Status:   closed
Priority: 1
Labels:   kind:bug,  area:routing,  area:provider-health,  impact:cost,  impact:reliability,  parent:fizeau-0bc93861, ac-quality:needs-refinement
Deps:     fizeau-458f10b0
Desc:     Second of three sub-beads for parent fizeau-0bc93861. Wires openrouter credential gating into service_routing.go using the engine plumbing landed in fizeau-458f10b0. No network — purely a string/env check. The bug surfaces when api_key in fizeau config is the literal string '${OPENROUTER_API_KEY}' because the env var was not set during config load (see internal/config/config_test.go:258-266 which shows the literal string is preserved verbatim). Currently this bypasses ConfigError, the provider survives routing-input construction, openrouter is picked, and the dispatch fails with HTTP 401. This bead makes that case appear in routing_decision as eligible=false + filter_reason=credential_missing without burning a dispatch.

Scope:
- Detect 'no usable key': empty, contains the literal substring '${' (failed env expansion), or otherwise zero-length after TrimSpace. Mark the provider in ProviderEligibilityOverrides with credential_missing.
- Do not introduce any HTTP call. No probe state.
- Does not handle remote-derived states (credit_exhausted, credential_invalid, provider_unreachable) — those land in fizeau-XXC.

Depends on fizeau-458f10b0.
Accept:   1. With openrouter provider configured with api_key='${OPENROUTER_API_KEY}' and the env var unset, routing_decision exposes the openrouter candidate(s) with eligible=false and FilterReason=credential_missing and no HTTP request is issued to openrouter.ai. Verified by an integration test in service_*_test.go (package fizeau) that uses fakeServiceConfig with api_key='' (or the unexpanded placeholder) and asserts the candidates payload after running Execute (or ResolveRoute, whichever exposes Candidates) with no model pin.
2. With api_key set to a non-empty, non-placeholder value the gate is silent: candidate appears with eligible=true (still subject to other gates). Verified by a separate test case in the same file.
3. cd /Users/erik/Projects/fizeau && go test ./... green.
4. No regression in service_adr005_test.go or service_routing_test.go.

ID:       fizeau-d6bed010
Title:    openrouter health gate: credit-balance probe with TTL cache; surface credit_exhausted, credential_invalid, provider_unreachable
Type:     task
Status:   closed
Priority: 1
Labels:   kind:bug,  area:routing,  area:provider-health,  impact:cost,  impact:reliability,  parent:fizeau-0bc93861, ac-quality:needs-refinement
Deps:     fizeau-458f10b0, fizeau-73a00a6b
Desc:     Third of three sub-beads for parent fizeau-0bc93861. Adds the network-probe half of the openrouter health gate built on the engine plumbing from fizeau-458f10b0 and credential-check from fizeau-73a00a6b. Calls openrouter GET /api/v1/credits with a per-provider in-memory cache (configurable TTL, default 5 minutes) and projects results into ProviderEligibilityOverrides at routing-input build time.

State machine:
- HTTP 200, balance >= threshold → no override (eligible).
- HTTP 200, balance <  threshold (default $0.50) → credit_exhausted; embed observed balance in the candidate Reason text.
- HTTP 401 → credential_invalid (distinct from credential_missing).
- Transport failure / non-2xx other than 401 → provider_unreachable; record probe time; next routing pass eligible for a fresh probe.

Refresh follows the existing local-aliveness pattern (requestLocalHealthRefreshForRouting): synchronous routing pass reads cached state only; staleness triggers an async refresh goroutine. No background ticker.

Configurable: threshold ($0.50 default) and TTL (5m default) plumbed through ServiceOptions or ServiceConfig provider entry. Documented in docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-005-provider-config.md or the dedicated openrouter section (whichever houses the openrouter provider config docs).

Depends on fizeau-458f10b0 and fizeau-73a00a6b.
Accept:   1. With api_key valid and the stubbed /api/v1/credits returning {data:{total_credits: 0}} (or current zero-balance shape), routing_decision exposes openrouter candidate with eligible=false, FilterReason=credit_exhausted, Reason text contains the observed balance. Integration test via httptest.NewServer.
2. With api_key valid and the stubbed /api/v1/credits returning a balance > threshold, candidate is eligible=true. Verified by the existing happy-path test pattern (no regression).
3. /api/v1/credits is called at most once per TTL window across N back-to-back routing decisions. Verified by atomic counter in the stub and N=3 calls.
4. A transport failure (server.Close before the first probe) produces FilterReason=provider_unreachable for the freshness window; a follow-up routing pass after the stub recovers produces eligible=true with a fresh probe. Test asserts both observations.
5. HTTP 401 from /api/v1/credits surfaces as FilterReason=credential_invalid (distinct from credential_missing from sibling bead).
6. Configurable threshold for credit_exhausted is documented; default value ($0.50) named in the docs. File path: docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-005-provider-config.md OR a new file under the openrouter provider docs section.
7. cd /Users/erik/Projects/fizeau && go test ./... green.
8. No regression in service_adr005_test.go or service_routing_test.go.

verification_command: go test ./...

Parent fizeau-0bc93861 was decomposed (notes field) into three closed children whose merged commits land every parent AC:

- fizeau-458f10b0 (commit a277d14d, merge a64ea190) — adds FilterReason constants credential_missing, credential_invalid, credit_exhausted, provider_unreachable and the generic ProviderEligibilityOverrides plumbing in internal/routing/engine.go. Covers AC plumbing for AC1/AC2/AC5/AC6.
- fizeau-73a00a6b (commit 317af8e0, merge 983ff04e) — wires openrouter credential_missing in service_routing.go for empty/unexpanded ${...} api_key, with integration tests. Covers AC1.
- fizeau-d6bed010 (commits including eb193cd5, merge b0968160) — adds service_openrouter_credit.go credits probe with configurable TTL cache, surfaces credit_exhausted (with observed balance in Reason), credential_invalid (401), and provider_unreachable (transport failure with recovery on next pass). Documents the default $0.50 threshold in docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-005-provider-config.md. Tests in service_openrouter_credit_test.go and service_openrouter_credit_failure_modes_test.go cover AC2/AC4/AC5/AC6/AC7.

AC3 (happy-path eligible=true, no regression) and AC9 (no regression in service_adr005_test.go / service_routing_test.go) are covered by the full suite passing. AC8 (go test ./... green) is the verification_command above; just re-run from /Users/erik/Projects/fizeau and observed all packages ok including github.com/easel/fizeau and internal/routing.

No new code in this execution worktree — the parent has nothing left to do beyond its children. The verification_command exits 0 today, closing the parent.
